user_query:
You're our HR ops lead. Build a 12‑month workforce plan for our SaaS company using the files in input/. Read input/company_targets.yaml, input/current_workforce.csv, input/skills_inventory.json, input/succession_critical_roles.json, and input/cost_assumptions.json. Deliver these artifacts:

1) Write output/workforce_plan.json with: executive_summary; demand_forecasting using three methods (revenue_per_employee with assumed_rpe and required_headcount; driver_based with explicit Support and Engineering calculations, formulas, and results; managerial_span with target spans and managers_needed); department_plans for Engineering, Sales, Support, Marketing, and G&A including for each: current_FTEs, current_contractors, demand_forecast_headcount, pipeline_hires, headcount_gap, skills_to_develop, automation_opportunities (with estimated_savings), and a budget_summary; company- and department-level gap_analysis; contingent_workforce target_mix (FTE, contractors, outsourced, AI agents); succession_pipeline summary; and risks_and_mitigations.

2) Write output/hiring_plan.csv with columns: department, role, type (FTE/contractor), quarter, priority_score (use the priority formula weights provided), and cost_estimate_fully_loaded. Include at least one line per department from input/current_workforce.csv.

3) Write output/quarterly_review.md outlining the Q1–Q4 cadence and answering the 5 review questions per quarter with a green/yellow/red status and brief rationale.

4) Write output/succession_risks.json listing each critical role with successors (and readiness) and a summary count of roles with ready‑now successors.

Make all assumptions explicit in the JSON and markdown, show the numbers pulled from inputs, and keep percentages and counts internally consistent across files.
